title: LLM practical scale proxy
hypothesis: The current practical LLM proxy shows no nm2 latency benefit up to 32 active tokens and 512 KV-context tokens. A bounded larger proxy with 64 active tokens and 1024-2048 KV-context score dimensions should check whether that conclusion holds as LLM-like attention pressure grows.

evaluation.primary_question: Does the larger practical decoder proxy change scheduler visibility, softmax occupancy, or the fp16_nm1 vs fp16_nm2 PPA ranking?
scope: existing fp16_nm1 and fp16_nm2 physical points
64 active-token proxy cases with 1024 and 2048 KV-context score widths
out_of_scope: changing the bf16/PWL decoder quality path

promote_if: larger scale exposes a clear nm2 latency or balanced-PPA benefit
softmax occupancy, waits, or backpressure materially increase versus llm_practical_v1
iterate_if: fp16_nm1 remains best and no scheduler pressure appears
scale increases latency/energy proportionally without changing the architecture bottleneck

risks: still an ONNX-lite proxy rather than a trained decoder model
larger generated models may increase evaluator runtime
only compares currently materialized nm1 and nm2 array points
